sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 47-year-old male, presents with a 2-hour history of lower back pain and numbness in the right foot, which began after raking leaves in the yard.
He reports that the pain worsened when standing up.
He denies any previous similar episodes, except for one 3-year-old episode that resolved within a day.
He tried icing and taking 2 Advil, but it did not provide relief.
His partner reports that the patient's pain worsens when standing up, but not when bending over.
Past Medical History: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of Systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current Medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital Signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical Exam: The patient has some pain on flexion of the low back and tenderness to palpation around the L5 area.
Dorsiflexion is normal, and pulses are equal in all extremities.
Test Results: The patient had a normal X-ray of the low back.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely sprained his low back.
Problem list: Low back sprain; [Condition] [Status: active]; [Condition] [Status: active]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5mg PO QD for pain management.
I also prescribed physical therapy to help improve mobility and strength.
I want the patient to rest the affected area and follow up with me in two weeks to reassess the condition.
If the pain persists, we will consider ordering an MRI.
Follow-up: I want the patient to follow up with me in two weeks to reassess the condition and consider further imaging if necessary.
